dependencies: Critical components manufactured internally containing proprietary technology
Wide range of suppliers for electronic components
Single supplier reliance for certain components
Global supply arrangements for electronic components
risks: Global supply chain and production issues
Disruption of key manufacturing facilities in China, Europe, and United States
Limited alternate facilities for many products
Regional logistics centers with outsourced logistics activities
Supply chain disruptions from pandemics, strikes, labor shortages, power interruptions, cybersecurity attacks, natural disasters
Disruption of material sources from supplier operational issues or shipping delays
Quality and regulatory requirements for alternative materials
Time required to establish new supplier relationships
Shipping delays at European Logistics facility

regulatory_risks: Weights and measures regulatory standards and approvals
Electrical safety standards for electrical components
Legal for trade applications requiring specific approvals
Good Laboratory Practices (GLP) for laboratory customers
Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) for industrial customers
U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations for pharmaceutical customers
Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Point (HACCP) regulations for food processing
Special requirements for products used in hazardous environments
Environmental laws and regulations in operating jurisdictions
Compliance with design, development, product safety, labeling, manufacturing, promotion, sales, and distribution regulations
Competition, employment and labor practices, international trade, and corruption laws
Product recalls and manufacturing cessation for non-compliance
21 CFR Part 11 compliance for electronically stored data
Import, export control, and economic sanctions laws
European General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
Cybersecurity, privacy, and data protection regulations
Conflict minerals disclosure and reporting requirements (SEC)
Human rights and sustainable sourcing supply chain requirements
Climate change and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions regulations
Renewable energy and sustainability mandates
Additional taxes on fuel and energy
U.S. Supreme Court overturning Chevron doctrine affecting regulatory frameworks
Artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning regulations
Increased regulatory compliance expense from new laws

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions on Russian entities following Ukraine invasion
Export control and economic sanctions laws affecting transactions
Prohibition of exports of certain products, services, and technologies
Export license requirements for controlled items
Civil and criminal actions, monetary and non-monetary penalties for non-compliance
Disruptions to business and limitations on import/export abilities
U.S., European Union, and other countries imposed sanctions on Russia
Suspended shipments to Russia since February 2022
